display.purpose: Mono. The one display moment per screen: the `ezpds ▸ <prompt>` console line and the wordmark. Mono is the signet here — elevated, not relegated.

headline.purpose: Grotesk 600, ~1.375rem. Section and screen headings — the working voice.

title.purpose: Grotesk 600, ~1.125rem. Card titles, group headers, a device label.

body.purpose: Grotesk 400, 1rem, line-height 1.6 (looser than Obsign — light-on-dark reads lighter). Prose and explanations; cap at 65–75ch.

label.purpose: Grotesk 500, 0.8125rem, +0.01em. Buttons, field labels, metadata. Sentence case, never all-caps eyebrows.

data.purpose: Mono, 0.875rem. Claim codes, did:keys, device IDs, timestamps, status values — anything literal and verifiable. Wraps break-all; never truncates a code silently.

shadows.name: sheet
shadows.purpose: A confirmation sheet rising from the bottom — the only bottom-anchored elevation.

shadows.name: toast
shadows.purpose: Transient status notifications. Soft, neutral, never colored.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Ease-out (quart-like) default for every state transition. No bounce, no elastic.

motion.name: duration-fast
motion.purpose: Micro-feedback: copy confirm, focus ring, color shifts.

motion.name: duration-base
motion.purpose: Default control transitions (button background, toggle thumb).

motion.name: duration-slow
motion.purpose: Larger surface moves; still well under the 250ms product ceiling.

breakpoints.name: mobile
breakpoints.value: ≤430px — single-column iPhone WKWebView. Layout is fixed-column, full-height (100dvh); there are no width breakpoints. The only responsive switch is prefers-reduced-motion (the spinner/thumb hold still rather than disappear).

components.name: Button — Primary (One Lamp)
components.description: Sealing-Wax Gold fill with deep-slate ink (7.7:1, AAA). The single primary action per screen — the One Lamp. Used at most once.

components.name: Button — Secondary (quiet default)
components.description: Panel Slate with a Steel Line hairline. The quiet default action; carries everything that isn't the One Lamp.

components.name: Button — Destructive
components.description: Alarm Solid fill with light text (7.5:1, AAA). Reserved for the irreversible revoke / unpair — deliberately scarce.

components.name: StatusChip (tonal-pair badge)
components.description: Status is never color alone: each chip is a signal tone on a deep same-hue surface PLUS a terminal glyph PLUS a text label. The glyph is the register's voice; the label is the screen-reader truth.
components.html: ● active◌ pending⊘ revoked○ info

components.name: CodeOutput (signature component)
components.description: The signature surface: a claim code / did:key / device ID rendered as copyable terminal output — mono on Raised Slate, a leading gold ▸ prompt glyph, copy + share affordances. Wraps break-all; never truncates a literal silently (Literal-Truth rule).
components.html:
Account claim code
▸PDS-7Q4M-2XK9
components.css: .ds-code-frame{background:oklch(0.18 0.012 250);padding:24px;border-radius:10px;display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:4px}.ds-code-cap{font-family:system-ui, -apple-system, 'Helvetica Neue', sans-serif;font-size:0.8125rem;font-weight:500;color:oklch(0.73 0.010 250)}.ds-code-surface{display:flex;align-items:flex-start;gap:8px;background:oklch(0.26 0.015 250);border:1px solid oklch(0.34 0.012 250);border-radius:6px;padding:8px 16px}.ds-code-val{flex:1;min-width:0;font-family:'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', 'Menlo', monospace;font-size:0.875rem;line-height:1.55;color:oklch(0.95 0.006 250);overflow-wrap:anywhere;word-break:break-all}.ds-code-prompt{color:oklch(0.72 0.12 80);margin-right:8px}.ds-code-tools{flex:none;display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:4px}.ds-code-tool{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:4px;min-height:32px;padding:4px 8px;background:transparent;border:1px solid oklch(0.52 0.012 250);border-radius:4px;color:oklch(0.82 0.008 250);font-family:'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', 'Menlo', monospace;font-size:0.8125rem;cursor:pointer;transition:color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1),border-color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1)}.ds-code-tool:hover{color:oklch(0.95 0.006 250);border-color:oklch(0.73 0.010 250)}.ds-code-tool:focus-visible{outline:2px solid oklch(0.72 0.12 80);outline-offset:2px}.ds-code-tool svg{flex:none}

components.name: TextField
components.description: A labelled input well: Raised Slate ground, Steel Line border, 2px gold focus ring. The `mono` variant sets literal values (relay URL, pairing code) in JetBrains Mono. Errors are border + leading ! glyph + message, never color alone.
components.css: .ds-field-frame{background:oklch(0.18 0.012 250);padding:24px;border-radius:10px;display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:4px}.ds-field-label{font-family:system-ui, -apple-system, 'Helvetica Neue', sans-serif;font-size:0.8125rem;font-weight:500;color:oklch(0.82 0.008 250)}.ds-field-input{width:100%;min-height:44px;padding:8px 16px;background:oklch(0.26 0.015 250);color:oklch(0.95 0.006 250);border:1px solid oklch(0.52 0.012 250);border-radius:6px;font-family:'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', 'Menlo', monospace;font-size:0.875rem;transition:border-color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1)}.ds-field-input:focus-visible{outline:2px solid oklch(0.72 0.12 80);outline-offset:2px;border-color:oklch(0.72 0.12 80)}

components.name: Toggle (switch)
components.description: A labelled switch for a boolean setting. State is conveyed by thumb POSITION and an explicit on/off mono label — never color alone. The gold “on” track is the active-state use of the brass accent, not a status color.
components.html: Require Face ID to signConfirm before generating a claim code or unpairing.
components.css: .ds-tog-frame{background:oklch(0.18 0.012 250);padding:24px;border-radius:10px;display:flex;align-items:flex-start;justify-content:space-between;gap:16px}.ds-tog-text{display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:2px;min-width:0}.ds-tog-label{font-family:system-ui, -apple-system, 'Helvetica Neue', sans-serif;font-size:1rem;font-weight:500;color:oklch(0.95 0.006 250)}.ds-tog-desc{font-family:system-ui, -apple-system, 'Helvetica Neue', sans-serif;font-size:0.8125rem;color:oklch(0.82 0.008 250)}.ds-tog{flex:none;display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;min-height:44px;padding:0;background:transparent;border:none;cursor:pointer}.ds-tog-state{font-family:'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', 'Menlo', monospace;font-size:0.8125rem;color:oklch(0.72 0.12 80);min-width:2ch;text-align:right}.ds-tog-track{position:relative;display:inline-block;width:40px;height:24px;background:oklch(0.72 0.12 80);border:1px solid oklch(0.72 0.12 80);border-radius:9999px}.ds-tog-thumb{position:absolute;top:50%;left:4px;width:16px;height:16px;margin-top:-8px;background:oklch(0.16 0.012 250);border-radius:9999px;transform:translateX(16px);transition:transform 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1)}.ds-tog:focus-visible{outline:2px solid oklch(0.72 0.12 80);outline-offset:2px;border-radius:4px}

components.name: ScreenShell prompt (console header)
components.description: The screen scaffold's display moment: the `ezpds ▸ <prompt>` line in mono — the one place the display face appears, once per screen — over a working-voice grotesk headline. Keeps every operator screen structurally identical.
components.html:
ezpds▸claim code

Generate a claim code

components.name: DeviceRow (the ls -l row)
components.description: A dense, aligned ls -l row: label + shortened mono did:key (head…tail with a VISIBLE ellipsis, the full value one tap away) + last-seen, with a StatusChip on the right. The operator's current device is marked by a gold “this device” label, not a side-stripe.
components.html:
iPhone 17 Pro this devicedid:key:zDnaerByL1n8…aH2g
● activeseen 2m ago
components.css: .ds-row-frame{background:oklch(0.18 0.012 250);padding:24px;border-radius:10px}.ds-row{display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;gap:16px;padding:16px;background:oklch(0.22 0.014 250);border:1px solid oklch(0.34 0.012 250);border-radius:10px}.ds-row-main{display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:4px;min-width:0}.ds-row-label{font-family:system-ui, -apple-system, 'Helvetica Neue', sans-serif;font-size:1.125rem;font-weight:600;color:oklch(0.95 0.006 250);display:flex;gap:8px;align-items:baseline}.ds-row-self{font-family:system-ui, -apple-system, 'Helvetica Neue', sans-serif;font-size:0.8125rem;font-weight:500;color:oklch(0.72 0.12 80)}.ds-row-id{font-family:'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', 'Menlo', monospace;font-size:0.875rem;color:oklch(0.82 0.008 250)}.ds-row-meta{flex:none;display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:flex-end;gap:4px}.ds-chip{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:4px;padding:4px 8px;border-radius:4px;font-family:'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', 'Menlo', monospace;font-size:0.8125rem;line-height:1;white-space:nowrap}.ds-chip-g{font-size:0.9em}.ds-chip-safe{color:oklch(0.82 0.10 150);background:oklch(0.26 0.04 150)}.ds-row-seen{font-family:'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', 'Menlo', monospace;font-size:0.8125rem;color:oklch(0.73 0.010 250)}

narrative.overview:
This is the control surface of a precision instrument: brass dials and gold indicator lamps lit against cool brushed steel, in a calm, dimly-lit operations room. The operator who self-hosts a relay administers it today from a terminal; this is that terminal made into an instrument they can hold — exact, dense, and fast, the way good operator tooling (Warp, Charm, Linear, Railway) is exact. Gravitas comes from precision and legible density — aligned columns, monospace where the data is literal, gold reserved for the one action that matters — never from chrome and never from theatrics.

It is the technical sibling of Obsign, the identity wallet. It shares Obsign's soul — the sealing-wax gold, the “practice the assurance you preach” rigor, the WCAG 2.2 AAA bar — but deliberately inverts the register: where Obsign is warm parchment under daylight, this is cool slate under low light; where Obsign hides the cryptographic machinery behind plain human stakes, this leads with it, because the machinery is the operator's native language. The two apps are recognizably the same house; this one is the operator's room in it.

This system explicitly rejects hacker cosplay / terminal kitsch (CRT scanlines, phosphor glow, Matrix rain, h4ck3r green-on-black, fake boot logs as decor), consumer-app friendliness (humane onboarding carousels, soft playful cards, mascots, reassurance copy — Obsign's lane, not this one), crypto / web3 hype (neon, coins, gradients), the enterprise dashboard / chart-soup (gauge clusters, KPI hero cards), and the low-contrast dark-theme cliché (gray-on-darker-gray mush). Terminal-native here means a refined command surface, not a costume.
keyCharacteristics: Cool slate ground, sealing-wax gold accent — brass on steel, a deliberate temperature/value inversion of Obsign's gold-on-white.
Monospace-forward: the mono is the signature voice (the literal truth of a code or key), a grotesk carries dense UI and prose.
Dense but legible — the clarity of a good ls -l, never chart-soup, never mush.
Gold is the one action, never decoration; status is the green/amber/red/slate ramp, never gold.
Status is never color alone — color + glyph + text + position, always.
WCAG 2.2 AAA, held against a dark ground where it is hardest — the one Obsign principle carried with zero compromise.

rules.name: The Seal-vs-Signal Rule
rules.body: Gold is action and identity; the green/amber/red/slate ramp is status. Status colors always carry a glyph and a text label and appear only in status contexts. Gold never masquerades as a status, and a status color never carries the brand. This rule is what lets a warm accent and a warm-ish amber coexist without confusion.

rules.name: The Cool-Ground Rule
rules.body: The surface stays cool slate (hue ~250). Warmth lives in the brass accent and nowhere else. Tinting the ground warm “to feel inviting” is forbidden — this is an instrument, not a hearth, and it muddies AAA contrast.

rules.name: The One-Lamp Rule
rules.body: Brass appears on ≤10% of any screen — ideally one element: the primary action or the current selection. A console with every control lit gold is a console no operator can read. Its rarity is what makes it read as the action.

rules.name: The Literal-Truth Rule
rules.body: Every code, did:key, device ID, and signature is set in mono. Monospace signals “this is exact, verify it character by character.” Proportional type is for prose, never for literals.

rules.name: The Mono-as-Voice, Not-Costume Rule
rules.body: Mono is elevated because this audience is technical and reads literal output — not as a decorative “developer” skin. The instant mono is paired with scanlines, glow, or fake terminal chrome, it has become the kitsch this system bans. Refined command surface, never costume.

rules.name: The Flat-At-Rest Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat with a hairline at rest. A shadow is a response to floating (sheet, toast) — a state, not a decoration. A panel with a resting shadow is wrong.

dos: Do keep the ground cool slate (oklch(0.18 0.012 250)) and let brass and type carry the warmth.
Do treat gold as the One Lamp — the single primary action or current selection, ≤10% of the screen. Matte brass, never metallic glint.
Do hold WCAG 2.2 AAA against the dark ground — Filament on Console Slate clears it; verify every text-on-color and every status pairing.
Do signal status with color and a glyph and a text label and position — always all four (● active / ⊘ revoked / ! error).
Do set every claim code, did:key, device ID, and signature in mono; wrap break-all, never truncate a literal silently.
Do lead with the literal — report exact output the way a terminal would, not soft consumer phrasing.
Do keep density legible: aligned columns, the clarity of a good ls -l.
Do keep a visible 2px gold focus ring on every interactive element.
Do stay flat at rest; reserve the two soft shadows for floating sheets and toasts.
donts: Don't do hacker cosplay / terminal kitsch — no CRT scanlines, phosphor glow, Matrix rain, h4ck3r green-on-black, or fake boot logs as decoration. Refined command surface, not costume.
Don't drift into consumer-app friendliness (Obsign's lane): no humane onboarding carousels, soft playful cards, mascots, emoji-led empty states, or reassurance copy.
Don't ship the low-contrast dark-theme cliché: no gray-on-darker-gray mush. If body text is even close to 7:1, push it toward Filament.
Don't let gold look like money — no metallic gradients, coin shapes, or glint. The instant it reads as currency it's the crypto/web3 anti-reference.
Don't build the enterprise dashboard / chart-soup: no gauge clusters, KPI hero cards, or SaaS analytics widgets. Density is legible operator output, not dashboards.
Don't let a status color carry the brand, or gold masquerade as a status (the Seal-vs-Signal Rule).
Don't signal urgency by color alone — a colorblind operator must never have to guess which device is live.
Don't use a border-left/border-right colored stripe; use a full 1px Steel Line hairline, a tonal ground, or a leading glyph.
Don't use gradient-clipped text, decorative glassmorphism, or a big-number hero-metric template.
